content.text:
```json { "module": "ui-design-system", "scores": { "visual_polish": 62, "domain_depth": 38, "data_richness": 35, "interaction_quality": 52, "ai_integration": 20, "mobile_first": 40, "performance": 55, "typescript_quality": 58, "error_handling": 55, "realtime": 30, "accessibility": 32, "security": 45, "differentiation": 42, "enterprise_readiness": 48 }, "overall_score": 44, "issues": [ { "id": "ui-design-system-C3-001", "severity": "critical", "dimension": "accessibility", "title": "Breadcrumb navigation buttons missing ARIA attributes and nav landmark", "file": "src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx", "description": "The FolderBreadcrumbs component renders breadcrumb buttons without a wrapping nav element with aria-label='Breadcrumb', without aria-current='page' on the last item, and without role='navigation'. Screen readers cannot identify this as breadcrumb navigation. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require all pages pass axe-core with zero critical violations.", "prompt": "In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component: wrap the entire breadcrumb output in a
element. Add an
    inside the nav, and wrap each breadcrumb segment in an
  1. element. On the last breadcrumb item (the current location), add aria-current=\"page\". Also in the route-based Breadcrumbs component (if present in the same file), apply the same nav > ol > li structure with aria-label=\"Breadcrumb\" and aria-current=\"page\" on the final segment. BEFORE: breadcrumb buttons are rendered as flat divs/buttons with no semantic nav landmark or list structure. AFTER: breadcrumbs are wrapped in
    1. ...
    with aria-current=\"page\" on the active item. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "ui-design-system-C3-002", "severity": "critical", "dimension": "error_handling", "title": "Toast error messages auto-dismiss countdown timer leaks intervals on unmount", "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx", "description": "In the ToastEntry component, the countdown interval (countdownIntervalRef) and fallback timer (fallbackTimerRef) are set up in a useEffect but the cleanup function only clears them on unmount. If the toast is manually dismissed before the interval fires, the interval reference may already be stale. Additionally, pause/resume on hover is not implemented, so error toasts with 60 second countdowns cannot be paused while the user reads them, violating the design standard that error toasts require manual dismissal.", "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry component: add onMouseEnter and onMouseLeave handlers to the toast container div. On mouseEnter, pause the auto-dismiss timer by clearing timerRef.current (and fallbackTimerRef.current for error toasts) and recording the elapsed time in remainingRef.current. On mouseLeave, restart the timer with the remaining duration. Also pause the countdown interval on hover by clearing countdownIntervalRef.current and resuming it on mouse leave. BEFORE: error toasts count down from 60 and cannot be paused; hovering has no effect on auto-dismiss timing. AFTER: hovering over any toast pauses its auto-dismiss timer and countdown; moving the mouse away resumes from where it left off. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "ui-design-system-C3-003", "severity": "major", "dimension": "mobile_first", "title": "Breadcrumb mobile detection uses window.innerWidth without resize listener cleanup guard", "file": "src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx", "description": "The FolderBreadcrumbs component initializes isMobile state from window.innerWidth but the code snippet shows the useState initializer without evidence of a resize event listener to update it. On tablets rotating from portrait to landscape (or vice versa), the breadcrumb layout will not adapt. The design standard requires every component be usable at 375px width with responsive breakpoints at 768px and 1280px.", "prompt": "In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component: verify that a useEffect with a resize listener exists that updates the isMobile state. If it does not exist, add one: useEffect(() => { const handler = () => setIsMobile(window.innerWidth < 768); window.addEventListener('resize', handler); return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', handler); }, []). On mobile (isMobile true), truncate the breadcrumb trail to show only the root and the current folder with an ellipsis button in between that expands to show the full path. Ensure all breadcrumb touch targets are at least 44x44px (already set via minHeight/minWidth in crumbButtonStyle). BEFORE: isMobile may be set once on mount and never updated on resize; full breadcrumb path always shown regardless of screen width. AFTER: isMobile updates on window resize; on mobile, breadcrumbs collapse to root + ellipsis + current with expandable full path. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "ui-design-system-C3-004", "severity": "major", "dimension": "visual_polish", "title": "Toast component missing entrance/exit animations per design standard", "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx", "description": "The design standards specify all transitions should be 150ms ease-out, and toast notifications should fade in and slide from the edge. The ToastEntry component renders without any entrance or exit animation, causing toasts to appear and disappear abruptly. This violates the design standard for professional animation polish and makes it easy for users to miss important notifications.", "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry component: add CSS transition-based entrance and exit animations. On mount, the toast should start with opacity 0 and transform translateY(16px), then animate to opacity 1 and translateY(0) over 150ms ease-out. Use a useState for mounted state that flips to true in a useEffect after a requestAnimationFrame. Before removal (onClose), animate to opacity 0 and translateY(-8px) over 150ms, then call the actual removal. Add a wrapper div with style={{ transition: 'opacity 150ms ease-out, transform 150ms ease-out', opacity: isVisible ? 1 : 0, transform: isVisible ? 'translateY(0)' : 'translateY(16px)' }}. BEFORE: toasts appear and disappear instantly with no animation. AFTER: toasts slide in from below with a fade, and fade out upward when dismissed, matching the 150ms ease-out design standard. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." }, { "id": "ui-design-system-C3-005", "severity": "major", "dimension": "accessibility", "title": "Toast notifications not announced to screen readers via aria-live region", "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx", "description": "The ToastProvider renders toast items visually but does not use an aria-live region to announce new toasts to screen readers. When an RFI is created or an error occurs, sighted users see the toast but screen reader users receive no notification. The PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require all status changes announced via aria-live regions and zero critical axe-core violations.", "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastProvider component: wrap the toast container div with role=\"status\" and aria-live=\"polite\" for success/info/warning toasts. For error toasts, use role=\"alert\" and aria-live=\"assertive\" so they interrupt the screen reader. The simplest approach is to add a single container div with aria-live=\"polite\" role=\"status\" that holds all toasts, and for error severity toasts, add role=\"alert\" on the individual toast div. Also ensure each toast dismiss button has aria-label=\"Dismiss notification\". BEFORE: toasts render visually only; screen readers are not notified of new toast messages. AFTER: the toast container has aria-live=\"polite\" and role=\"status\"; error toasts have role=\"alert\" with aria-live=\"assertive\"; dismiss buttons have descriptive aria-labels. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors." } ], "invented_features": [], "competitive_intel": "Procore's notification system uses both in-app toast and email with WCAG AA accessible toast announcements. Fieldwire's mobile breadcrumb collapses elegantly to just the current context with a back chevron. SiteSync's toast and breadcrumb components are functional but lack the animation polish and accessibility rigor of Linear or Stripe's design systems, which both use aria-live regions for all transient UI and smooth 150ms transitions on every ephemeral element.", "summary": "The UI Design System module scores 44/100, held back primarily by accessibility gaps (missing ARIA landmarks on breadcrumbs, no aria-live on toasts), lack of animation polish on toast entrance/exit, and mobile responsiveness issues in breadcrumb navigation. The toast system has a solid severity-based architecture and error countdown feature, but timer pause-on-hover is missing and screen reader users are completely excluded from toast notifications. The breadcrumb component uses proper theme tokens and touch-friendly sizing but lacks semantic HTML structure (nav/ol/li) required for assistive technology. Fixing the five identified issues would bring accessibility from 32 to approximately 55, visual polish from 62 to 70, and mobile-first from 40 to 50, pushing the overall score toward 52. All fixes are surgical, targeting exactly one file each, and focus on standards compliance rather than new features." } ```
